insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument. This allows developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. The change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to utilize the new @inContext directive with the channelId argument. This will enable them to provide more accurate and channel-specific data to merchants. The change is backward compatible, so existing queries will still work, but developers should test their applications with the new API version to ensure proper functionality. The GraphQL Admin API and other APIs remain unaffected.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and channel-specific data, which can lead to improved sales and customer experiences. The change does not require any action from merchants, as it is handled by developers and the Storefront API.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying sales channel data, which is essential for merchants to manage their sales channels effectively. It also demonstrates Shopify's commitment to improving the Storefront API and supporting developer needs.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various objects and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, simplifying the process of building merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is now available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for your app to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, providing more detailed cart line item data.
CLI version 4.5.1 bumps cli-hydrogen to 13.0.1, fixing issues and improving stability.

recap.headline: Shopify updates: new events, Liquid templates, and API enhancements

themes.title: Events and Webhooks
themes.detail: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for Order, Collection, InventoryItem, and more. The Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId` for applying sales channel context to queries.

themes.title: Theme and App Development
themes.detail: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building. The physical inventory feature preview introduces new inventory APIs in the unstable GraphQL Admin API. POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components.

themes.title: Security and Compliance
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, enhancing security for apps storing cardholder data. App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps, reducing merchant confusion.

actions.text: Update apps to use new events and metafield triggers
Test physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Ensure app names are unique to comply with App Store requirements 4.1.2
Implement mTLS client certificate for card deposit endpoint
